oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Phenotypic and genotypic antimicrobial susceptibility of non-aureus staphylococci in dairy goats
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Buckley, Michelle
Sattar, Fazal
Li, Ganwu
Sahin, Orhan
Busch, Roselle
Kreuder, Amanda
Plummer, Paul
Gorden, Patrick
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Subclinical mastitis, most commonly due to non-aureus staphy­lococci (NAS), results in significant production losses within the dairy goat industry while also shortening the productive life of the animal due to the resultant chronic changes within the udder. The prevalence of this group of pathogens may be up to 50% in some dairies. Dry-off is a common production point to address these infections through the extra-label use of long-act­ing intramammary antimicrobial products which are approved for dairy cattle in the U.S. However, the impacts of this treat­ment on antimicrobial resistance development are unknown in goats. The aim of this study was to evaluate the impact of tar­geted dry therapy on phenotypic and genotypic antimicrobial resistance of NAS in dairy goats.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 268
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
